test.command: uv run pytest -m "not integration_local and not integration_provider" -x -q

typecheck.observation: 109 warnings (all non-blocking), 0 errors. Warnings include invalid-argument-type in test files, unresolved-attribute on BaseAgent.max_turns, unresolved-import for pandas, and not-subscriptable on Optional returns. All in test code, not production code.

nonBlockingIssues.description: Dead code: _PROVIDER_ENV_KEYS dict in registry.py is defined but never referenced
Implicit httpx dependency via openai package — httpx should be declared directly
New httpx.Client created per request (no connection pooling)
Pricing database has hardcoded rates 'as of early 2024', several models outdated
Helper functions duplicated between rollup.py and aggregation.py (DRY violation)
Custom statistical implementation (chi-squared) could use scipy, but scipy is intentionally not a dependency
avg_duration_ms computation semantics could be clearer (divides equally among tools)
HTML export uses string.format() — future string fields could be missed in HTML escaping
README links to LICENSE file that does not exist in the repository
Empty 'synthesis' optional dependency group in pyproject.toml — leftover from tree-sitter move
test_no_conflicting_short_flags is a no-op test (body is just 'pass')
test_run_help_shows_output_format_flag uses disjunction that is overly permissive
_VALID_CONFIG constant defined but never used in test_cross_cli_workflow.py
import io at bottom of file instead of top-level imports section
test_no_provider_specific_fields_in_report uses exact field matching — brittle if new fields added
InterruptibleExperiment uses 'agent: Any' and 'env: Any' consistent with pre-existing Harness pattern
SIGTERM handler logs total_tasks as len(self._results) instead of actual total
features.json declares fulfills VAL-CROSS-06/VAL-CROSS-11 but tests also cover VAL-CROSS-10

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document that 'dict[str, Any]' is an accepted pattern for heterogeneous result/telemetry dicts in reporting and export modules, despite the general guidance to avoid Any types. The type erasure is intentional since these modules process dynamic data from multiple sources.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: m6-reporting-and-export and m6-cross-error-propagation reviews both note extensive use of dict[str, Any] in reporting/aggregation/comparison/export code. This is architecturally appropriate for heterogeneous data processing but conflicts with AGENTS.md anti-Any guidance.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the cross-cutting test naming convention: test_cross_<feature>.py for integration/validation tests that verify behavior across multiple subsystems. This pattern is well-established with 7+ files but not documented.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: m6-cross-cli-workflow review identified the naming pattern across test_cross_eval_export.py, test_cross_schema_consistency.py, test_cross_agent_telemetry.py, test_cross_optimization.py, test_cross_tool_binding.py, test_cross_provider_report.py, test_cross_cli_workflow.py, and test_cross_error_propagation.py.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify that the harness intentionally runs test commands after agent errors for non-TIMEOUT/SKIPPED statuses — the test result is authoritative over the agent error. Workers writing error propagation tests should know this design decision.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: m6-cross-error-propagation review: the worker had to adjust test expectations after discovering agent exception + passing test = SUCCESS. Harness code: should_run_tests = agent_result.status not in (TIMEOUT, SKIPPED).

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a note in TDD procedure that cross-cutting validation features (which verify pre-existing code rather than implementing new code) may skip the 'red phase' since tests will often pass on first run.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both m6-cross-cli-workflow and m6-cross-multi-provider-aggregation reviews noted this deviation from TDD procedure. Workers correctly identified that validation features test existing code, so tests pass immediately.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the CLI flag naming inconsistency: 'run' uses --output-format/-o for format specification while 'report' and 'export' use --format. This is intentional (to avoid ambiguity with --output on run), but the rationale is not documented.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: m6-cross-cli-workflow review documented that run uses --output-format because --output is already used for output file path. In report/export, -o short form is used for --output (output file path), not format.

rejectedObservations.observation: Worker scoped type checking to src/grist_mill/providers/ instead of full repo (m6-llm-providers review)
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented: AGENTS.md already instructs workers to scope ty check to src/grist_mill/ only. The m6-package-polish feature already configured ty.src in pyproject.toml to handle this globally.

rejectedObservations.observation: Reports and export modules use subpackage structure (reports/, export/) which conflicts with AGENTS.md flat module guidance (m6-reporting-and-export review)
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented: The AGENTS.md architecture diagram already shows reports/ as a directory. This is an accepted exception for modules with clear internal cohesion.

rejectedObservations.observation: Worker deviated from skill by moving openai from optional extras to core dependencies (m6-llm-providers review)
rejectedObservations.reason: already-addressed: This was a justified deviation documented by the worker. The openai package is needed for its transitive httpx dependency.

rejectedObservations.observation: Worker added filterwarnings = ['ignore::DeprecationWarning'] to pytest config (m6-package-polish review)
rejectedObservations.reason: duplicate: This is a minor config addition already in place. Not significant enough to warrant a guidance update.

rejectedObservations.observation: services.yaml test_grist_mill command uses manually maintained list of test files (m6-cross-cli-workflow review)
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous: The test_all command already uses the marker-based pattern. The test_grist_mill manual list exists for a specific subset but maintenance burden is low since the test_all command covers the standard case.
